footer.mission: Engineering is the foundation on which Uzbekistan builds its future.
intro.step1: Drag the plug to the socket — or tap the plug, then the socket
about.missionBody: The National Engineering Society of Uzbekistan exists to advance the practice of engineering in the public interest — setting professional standards, certifying competence, and giving the country's engineers a single national voice.
about.whoBody: Practising engineers, engineering students, universities, and the industrial enterprises that employ them — across every region of Uzbekistan, from Tashkent to Nukus.
about.historyBody: NESU was established as a national professional body uniting the previously separate discipline societies under one charter, with headquarters in Tashkent and regional chapters in every viloyat.
gallery.hint: Click a photo to view it full size.
contact.sent: Thank you — your message has been received. The secretariat replies within three working days.
news.title: Engineering News
news.intro: What's happening in engineering worldwide — a curated digest for members.
assistant.title: NESU Assistant
assistant.greeting: Salom! I'm the NESU assistant. Ask me about membership, events, competitions or how to reach us.
assistant.a.join: Membership is open to practising engineers, students and firms. The quickest way: send your name and discipline via the Contact page, and the secretariat will send you an application form.
assistant.a.events: The next event is the Grid Modernisation Lecture on 21 Aug 2026 at NESU House, followed by the National Congress on 18–19 Sep. See the Events page for the full calendar.
assistant.a.contact: Write to info@nesu.uz, call +998 71 200-00-00, or use the form on the Contact page. The secretariat replies within three working days.
assistant.a.competitions: Two competitions are open now: the National Bridge Challenge (deadline 01 Oct 2026) and Code for Infrastructure (15 Nov 2026). Details on the Competitions page.
assistant.a.fallback: I don't have an answer for that yet — please leave your question via the Contact form and a member of staff will reply personally.
blackout.question: But, what is engineering?
activity.elec.instr: Close the loop: add the two missing wires, then flip the switch.
activity.elec.solved: It lights! The circuit is complete.
activity.sw.instr: This function should average an array of loads — but it returns NaN. Pick the change that fixes it.
activity.sw.correct: Fixed — the loop stays inside the array and the tests pass.
activity.sw.wrong: Not quite — that doesn't fix the failing read. Try again.
footer.glitchLine: The world doesn't need people who notice problems. It needs engineers who solve them.
home.contactLead: Questions, membership, partnership — the secretariat is one message away.
qa.q1: How do I become a member?
qa.a1: Fill in the application on the Contact page or write to info@nesu.uz. You'll need a diploma or student ID and one referee from the profession; the secretariat confirms within two weeks.
qa.a2: Standard membership is a modest annual fee, with a reduced rate for students and retirees. Corporate membership is priced by organisation size.
qa.a3: Open lectures and regional meetups are free to everyone. The National Congress and workshops are members-first, with guest places when capacity allows.
qa.q4: How do I propose a lecture or workshop?
qa.a4: Send a one-paragraph abstract to the secretariat. The programme committee reviews proposals monthly and pairs new speakers with an experienced session chair.
qa.q5: What is NESU, in one sentence?
qa.a5: NESU is the national professional body that unites Uzbekistan's engineers, sets standards, and develops the next generation of technical talent.
qa.q6: Is there a student chapter at my university?
qa.a6: Chapters operate at the major technical universities in Tashkent, Samarkand, Fergana and Nukus. If yours doesn't have one, five students and a faculty advisor are enough to start it.
testnote.title: This website is in test mode
testnote.body: The NESU website is still being tested. Pages may contain bugs, display errors, or wrong wording. If you notice anything broken or incorrect, please report it below — every report helps us fix the site faster.
testnote.sent: Thank you — your report has been received.
